## Product Description

Electrical Specification：

Operating voltage range: 5-8.4 Volts

Operating speed at no load ( 5V ): 0.11 sec/60°

Operating speed at no load ( 7.4V ): 0.075 sec/60°

Operating speed at no load ( 8.4V ): 0.065 sec/60°

Stall torque at locked ( 5V ): 26kg.cm(361oz-in)

Stall torque at locked ( 7.4V ): 35kg.cm(486oz-in)

Stall torque at locked ( 8.4V ): 39kg.cm(541.6oz-in)

Stall current at locked ( 5V ): 3.5A

Stall current at locked ( 7.4V ): 5.2A

Stall current at locked ( 8.4V ): 5.8A

Idle current at stopped (5/7.4/8.4V): 5mA

Control Specification:

Control System: PWM (Pulse width modification)

Pulse width range: 500-2500μsec

Neutral position: 1500μsec

Running degree: 270° (when 500-2500μsec)

Dead band width: 2μsec

Operating frequency: 50-330Hz

Rotating direction: Counterclockwise (When 1500-2500μsec)

Product Specification:

Suitable for: for 1/8 1/10 1/12 Scale RC Cars, Trucks

Motor Type：Coreless Motor

Gear Type: Stainless Steel

Spline:25 Tooth

Gear ratio: 195

Bearing：Double Bearing

Waterproof Performance：IP66

Connector Wire: 17.7in (450mm)

Size: 1.57\*0.79\*1.59inch（40 \* 20 \* 38.5mm）

Weight: 2.11oz (60g)

CE /RoHs: Yes

Apply Environmental Conditon:

Storage Temperature Range:-30°C~80°C

Operating Temperature Range:-15°C~70°C

Packing List:

1 x DS3225 PRO Servo (270°)

1 x 25T Adjustable Metal Servo Arm

1 x Accessories Set (As Shown on The Picture)

##### Great Servo for the price

I ordered this to go into an Arrma Senton Mega 4x4. It is a direct drop in replacement for the stock one. My steering response is much faster than the stock one. I usually run my Senton with street tires, so not a lot of dirt bashing or jumps. My son has the same truck, and his is setup with big monster truck tires. I will be putting one of these in his as he broke his cartwheeling his truck after a jump. I will update my review when that gets installed and after we bash it a while. Overall, great servo for the price, a direct drop in for an Arrma Senton, and will more than likely fit many other 1/10 RC vehicles.

It’s a good servo, especially for the price. I installed it in a Xmaxx, and while I was able to get it to fit, it did need an adapter, and a little bit of trimming on the mounting flanges. The power is great and the speed is good, especially with the huge tires on the Xmaxx. I would recommend just getting a Hot Racing 25T servo horn for the Xmaxx if you are going to use this servo. The one included does work, but doesn’t integrate as cleanly as the oem one, and lacks support. It is worth mentioning that your stock servo horn will not fit. My only complaint is that the cable is quite long, and can be hard to manage, especially on the Xmaxx platform.
